---
title: "Task 2: Summarize and visualize"
number-sections: false
---

[← Back to session page](../08_dplyr.qmd)

:::{.callout-note}

## Find the solution here after the session ends.

```{=html}
<script>const course_day = 2;</script>
<script src="../../timer_solution.js"></script>
<p class="r_class_solution" style="display:none">
  <a href="../solutions/dplyr-solution-2.html" target="_blank">Click here for the solution.</a>
</p>
```
:::

## Getting started

Before you start, make sure to load the `tidyverse` package.

```{r}
#| eval: false
library(tidyverse)
```

## Count

1. Count the number of penguins on each island.

2. Count the number of penguins of each species on each island.

## Summarize

3. Calculate mean flipper length and body mass for the 3 species separately.

4. Calculate mean flipper length and body mass by species and sex. Remove penguins with unknown sex first.

## Combine dplyr and ggplot

5. Remove penguins with missing `sex`, then pipe into a boxplot of body mass with `sex` on the x-axis.

6. Remove penguins with missing `sex`, then make a scatterplot of bill length vs. bill depth, colored by species.

## For the fast ones

- **A bit tricky:** Summarize mean body mass by species, then pipe the result into a bar chart with `geom_col()`. You haven't seen `geom_col()` yet but it works like other geoms: map species to `x` and mean body mass to `y`.

- Calculate the min, max, and mean flipper length per species.
  - **Extra**: Can you sort the result by mean flipper length? Hint: you'll need `arrange()` which we haven't covered yet. Check `?arrange` to figure out how it works.
